mechanism_narrative: The prevailing level of the federal funds rate sets the baseline discount rate applied to equity cash flows; higher observed levels mechanically increase the opportunity cost of capital and compress risk-asset present values, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of observations when FEDFUNDS is above 5%, or the mean forward return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) during such periods over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate of more than 0.3 percentage points over a three-month window signals deteriorating household income expectations and falling aggregate demand, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are non-negative in more than 55% of instances following a 0.3pp UNRATE increase, or the Sharpe ratio of the short signal is positive over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Weak retail sales coinciding with a restrictive federal funds rate above 4% jointly signal that monetary tightening is successfully constraining consumption expenditure, amplifying the negative impact on corporate revenues and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between RSAFS and FEDFUNDS fails to produce a statistically significant negative coefficient (p < 0.05) in predicting 20-day returns, or the strategy generates positive mean returns during the joint regime over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in housing starts during periods of low unemployment signal robust household formation and durable goods demand without immediate wage-push inflation, lifting cyclical equity returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST increases fail to predict positive 60-day S&P 500 returns when UNRATE is below 5%, or the hit rate of positive returns falls below 55% in this regime over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail sales directly captures the nominal spending impulse of the household sector and positively predicts near-term S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days through revenue expectation channels.
failure_condition: RSAFS changes show a rank correlation with subsequent 5-day S&P 500 returns below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive RSAFS changes predict negative mean returns.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate exceeding 5.0% places restrictive pressure on short-term liquidity and margin debt costs, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as leveraged positions are unwound.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns are positive in more than 50% of observations when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0%, or the mean forward return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) during triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production alongside stagnant payroll employment signals productivity-driven margin expansion rather than cost-push wage pressure, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of rising INDPRO and flat or falling PAYEMS fails to produce positive mean 60-day S&P 500 returns, or the interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) in a bivariate regression over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month contraction in nonfarm payrolls signals imminent recession risk and collapsing aggregate labor income, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are non-negative in more than 50% of months following a PAYEMS contraction, or the mean return during such months is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).
